learning_outcomes:
Learning outcome 1.  |  Major Programme:
By
successfully completing our major programme, the
sociology student should be able to:
Knowledge Domain
(1)  |  understand
the role of Sociology in contributing to our knowledge of social order and
its changes;
(2)  |  apply
basic concepts derived from sociological theories in the understanding of
social phenomenon;
(3)  |  pursue
an area of concentration (China studies, social research, and sociology and liberal
studies) whenever appropriate and prepare for his/her career development in
Chinese management, applied social research and teaching;
(4)  |  interpret
the developments of Chinese communities and their place in the global and
regional context;
Skills Domain
(5)  |  master
basic qualitative and quantitative methods in Sociology;
(6)  |  think
critically;
(7)  |  develop
transferable skills beyond disciplinary requirements;
Values Domain
(8)  |  reflect
on his or her own value position and appreciate:
·  |  sociology
as a useful and meaningful perspective on social reality and individual
biography;
·  |  the
importance of ethics, honesty, and transparency in research and academic
settings; and
·  |  the
importance of sociological analyses for reflection of value positions and
life choices.
2.  |  Sociology
and Journalism and Communication Double Major Programme:
Upon
completion of the Double Major Programme, students will have:
K
= Knowledge outcomes
(1)  |  gained
a breath of knowledge in Sociology and Journalism and Communication
(2)  |  developed
a high level of critical thinking and analytical reasoning skills through
cross-disciplinary training
(3)  |  expanded
and extended intellectual flexibility and diversity
(4)  |  developed
knowledge essential to fostering global and regional sustainable development
(5)  |  harnessed
the synergies between two influential social science disciplines
S
= Skills outcomes
(1)  |  developed
multiple skills in addressing complex world problems
(2)  |  acquired
the ability to theorize, design and execute social research critically and
independently
(3)  |  acquired
the ability to formulate evidence-based responses for policy or professional
communication
(4)  |  mastered
informative and persuasive communication and writing skills
(5)  |  acquired
analytical and innovative problem-solving abilities in preparation for
Industry 4.0
V
= Values and attitude outcomes
(1)  |  worked
under the mentoring of senior academics and qualified practitioners
(2)  |  developed
a strong sense of social consciousness, freedom of expression, and social
responsibility towards achieving sustainable development
(3)  |  acquired
the ability to instill a strong commitment to professional ethics in
communication professions and in conducting social research
(4)  |  developed
autonomy, responsibility and accountability in individual or collaborative
work
3.  |  Minor
Programme:
Upon the completion of the Minor Programme, students will have
demonstrated basic understanding of fundamental issues related to the subject,
acquired knowledge of some aspects of the subject with sufficient exposure
and at a suitable level to allow further study in this subject or related
subjects or to pursue a related career.

Sociology Sociology Applicable
to students admitted in 2023-24
Major
Programme Requirement
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 69 units of courses as follows:

Package:  |  9
SOCI1001
and any two courses, offered by two different

Explanatory
Notes:
1.  |  SOCI
courses at 2000 and above level will be included in the calculation of
Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Students
who pursue a second Major in Journalism and Communication can complete
COMM1120, SOCI1001 and one Faculty Package course offered by a Programme/Department/School
other than SOCI and COMM to satisfy the Faculty Package course
requirements of both Major Programmes.
3.  |  Students
who pursue a second Major in Journalism and Communication can be exempted
from taking COMM2160 by taking SOCI2003 as the
units earned can be counted towards both Major Programmes.
4.  |  Students
must have taken or registered for at least 27 units of COMM courses at
the last term of attendance before making declaration to take Journalism
and Communication as their second Major.
5.  |  Any
students should have taken at least 36 units of SOCI courses before
making official declaration to take the Journalism and Communication as
their second Major.
[a]  |  Sociology
Field Studies (SOCI3245)
-  |  The
field studies should have a strong component matching the theme of a particular
concentration in order to fulfill the requirement of that concentration
area.
-  |  If a student declares to take two concentration
areas, units earned from the course “Sociology Field Studies” will be
counted towards the fulfillment of the requirement of one area only,
subject to the course supervisor’s endorsement. The student should
indicate towards which area the units are counted when declaring the
concentrations.
-  |  If a student has taken SOCI3245 and 4211, only up to
3 units from either one course can be counted towards the fulfillment of
the concentration requirement, also subject to the course supervisor’s
endorsement.
[b]  |  Sociology
Internship: Nurturing Competency for Meeting Future Challenges (SOCI4211)
-  |  The
internship organisation and/or the internship job nature should have a
strong component matching the theme of a particular concentration in
order to fulfill the requirement of that concentration area.
-  |  If a student declares to take two concentration
areas, units earned from the course “Sociology Internship: Nurturing
Competency for Meeting Future Challenges” will be counted towards the
fulfillment of the requirement of one area only, subject to the course
supervisor’s endorsement. The student should indicate towards which area
the units are counted when declaring the concentrations.
-  |  If a student has taken SOCI3245 and 4211, only up to
3 units from either one course can be counted towards the fulfillment of
the concentration requirement, also subject to the course supervisor’s
endorsement.
[c]  |  Graduation Thesis II (SOCI4020)
-  |  The Graduation Thesis should have a strong component
matching the theme of a particular concentration in order to fulfill the
requirement of that concentration area.
-  |  If a student declares to take two concentration
areas, units earned from the course “Graduation Thesis II” will be
counted towards the fulfillment of the requirement of one area only,
subject to the course supervisor’s endorsement. The student should indicate
towards which area the units are counted when declaring the
concentrations.
[d]  |  Directed
Studies I/II (SOCI4410/4420)
-  |  The
Directed Studies should have a strong component matching the theme of a
particular concentration in order to fulfill the requirement of that
concentration area.
-  |  If
a student has taken SOCI4410 and 4420, and both are of the same theme
that can be counted towards the fulfillment of the requirement of one
concentration only, then only up to 3 units from either one course can be
counted towards the fulfillment of the concentration requirement, also
subject to the course supervisor’s endorsement.
-  |  If
a student has taken SOCI4410 and 4420 with different themes, subject to
the course supervisor’s endorsement, the units earned from SOCI4410 can
be counted towards the fulfillment of the requirement of one
concentration and SOCI4420 towards another.
-  |  Units
earned from a directed studies course can only be counted towards the fulfillment
of the requirement of one concentration.
[e]  |  Students are required to take either SOCI3227 or 3236
in order to fulfill the requirement of Required Courses. If a student has
taken both SOCI3227 and 3236, the extra 3 units earned can be counted
towards the fulfillment of the requirement of Elective Courses.
[f]  |  Students
are required to take either SOCI3002 or 3241 or 4204 in order to fulfill
the requirement of Required Courses. If a student has taken more than one
of these three courses, the extra 3 units or 6 units earned can be
counted towards the fulfillment of the requirement of Elective Courses.

Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  SOCI
courses at 2000 and above level will be included in the calculation of
Major GPA for honours classification.
[a]  |  Students
may apply for exemption of the course “SOCI1001 Introduction to Sociology”
if they have already taken similar subject during their Associate Degree
or Higher Diploma studies. But these students still have to complete a
minimum of 60 units (61 units for the concentration “Society and
Sustainable Development”) of Sociology courses for graduation. He/she
therefore will have to fulfill the major unit requirement by taking
another 3 units of Sociology elective course.
[b]  |  Sociology
Field Studies (SOCI3245)
-  |  The
field studies should have a strong component matching the theme of a
particular concentration in order to fulfill the requirement of that
concentration area.
-  |  If a student declares to take two concentration
areas, units earned from the course “Sociology Field Studies” will be
counted towards the fulfillment of the requirement of one area only,
subject to the course supervisor’s endorsement. The student should
indicate towards which area the units are counted when declaring the
concentrations.
-  |  If a student has taken SOCI3245 and 4211, only up to
3 units from either one course can be counted towards the fulfillment of
the concentration requirement, also subject to the course supervisor’s
endorsement.
[c]  |  Sociology
Internship: Nurturing Competency for Meeting Future Challenges (SOCI4211)
-  |  The
internship organisation and/or the internship job nature should have a
strong component matching the theme of a particular concentration in
order to fulfill the requirement of that concentration area.
-  |  If a student declares to take two concentration
areas, units earned from the course “Sociology Internship: Nurturing
Competency for Meeting Future Challenges” will be counted towards the
fulfillment of the requirement of one area only, subject to the course
supervisor’s endorsement. The student should indicate towards which area
the units are counted when declaring the concentrations.
-  |  If a student has taken SOCI3245 and 4211, only up to
3 units from either one course can be counted towards the fulfillment of
the concentration requirement, also subject to the course supervisor’s
endorsement.
[d]  |  Graduation Thesis II (SOCI4020)
-  |  The Graduation Thesis should have a strong component
matching the theme of a particular concentration in order to fulfill the requirement
of that concentration area.
-  |  If a student declares to take two concentration
areas, units earned from the course “Graduation Thesis II” will be
counted towards the fulfillment of the requirement of one area only,
subject to the course supervisor’s endorsement. The student should indicate
towards which area the units are counted when declaring the
concentrations.
[e]  |  Directed
Studies I/II (SOCI4410/4420)
-  |  The
Directed Studies should have a strong component matching the theme of a particular
concentration in order to fulfill the requirement of that concentration
area.
-  |  If
a student has taken SOCI4410 and 4420, and both are of the same theme
that can be counted towards the fulfillment of the requirement of one concentration
only, then only up to 3 units from either one course can be counted
towards the fulfillment of the concentration requirement, also subject to

-  |  If
a student has taken SOCI4410 and 4420 with different themes, subject to
the course supervisor’s endorsement, the units earned from SOCI4410 can
be counted towards the fulfillment of the requirement of one
concentration and SOCI4420 towards another.
-  |  Units
earned from a directed studies course can only be counted towards the
fulfillment of the requirement of one concentration.
[f]  |  Students are required to take either SOCI3227 or 3236
in order to fulfill the requirement of Required Courses. If a student has
taken both SOCI3227 and 3236, the extra 3 units earned can be counted
towards the fulfillment of the requirement of Elective Courses.
[g]  |  Students who opt for the concentration “Society,
Sustainability and Technology” are not allowed to declare the
concentration “Society and Sustainable Development”, and vice versa.
[h]  |  Students are required to take either SOCI3002 or 3241
or 4204 in order to fulfill the requirement of Required Courses. If a
student has taken more than one of these three courses, the extra 3 units
or 6 units earned can be counted towards the fulfillment of the
requirement of Elective Courses.
[i]  |  Students who wish to study in the concentration
“Society and Sustainable Development” should declare their intention in
the first term of the first year before course registration in
consultation with the Department of Sociology.
[j]  |  The units earned from courses offered by other
departments under this concentration can be counted towards the
fulfillment of major programme requirement.
[k]  |  Students are required to take either EESC2010 or
ESSC2010 or EESC2020 or ESSC2020 in order to fulfill the requirement of
Required Courses. If a student has taken more than one of these courses,
the extra units earned can be counted towards the fulfillment of the
requirement of Elective Courses.
[l]  |  Students are required to take either GDRS3005 or 3006
or 3007 or 3009 or 3015 in order to fulfill the requirement of Required
Courses. If a student has taken more than one of these courses, the extra
3 units earned can be counted towards the fulfillment of the requirement
of Elective Courses.

Explanatory
Notes:
1.  |  SOCI
courses at 2000 and above level will be included in the calculation of
Major GPA for honours classification.
[a]  |  Sociology Field
Studies (SOCI3245)
-  |  The field studies
should have a strong component matching the theme of a particular
concentration in order to fulfill the requirement of that concentration
area.
-  |  If a student declares
to take two concentration areas, units earned from the course “Sociology
Field Studies” will be counted towards the fulfillment of the requirement
of one area only, subject to the course supervisor’s endorsement. The
student should indicate towards which area the units are counted when
declaring the concentrations.
-  |  If a student has
taken SOCI3245 and 4211, only up to 3 units from either one course can be
counted towards the fulfillment of the concentration requirement, also
subject to the course supervisor’s endorsement.
[b]  |  Sociology
Internship: Nurturing Competency for Meeting Future Challenges (SOCI4211)
-  |  The internship
organisation and/or the internship job nature should have a strong
component matching the theme of a particular concentration in order to
fulfill the requirement of that concentration area.
-  |  If a student declares
to take two concentration areas, units earned from the course “Sociology
Internship: Nurturing Competency for Meeting Future Challenges” will be
counted towards the fulfillment of the requirement of one area only,
subject to the course supervisor’s endorsement. The student should
indicate towards which area the units are counted when declaring the
concentrations.
-  |  If a student has
taken SOCI3245 and 4211, only up to 3 units from either one course can be
counted towards the fulfillment of the concentration requirement, also
subject to the course supervisor’s endorsement.
[c]  |  Graduation Thesis
II (SOCI4020)
-  |  The Graduation Thesis
should have a strong component matching the theme of a particular
concentration in order to fulfill the requirement of that concentration
area.
-  |  If a student declares
to take two concentration areas, units earned from the course “Graduation
Thesis II” will be counted towards the fulfillment of the requirement of
one area only, subject to the course supervisor’s endorsement. The
student should indicate towards which area the units are counted when
declaring the concentrations.
[d]  |  Directed Studies
I/II (SOCI4410/4420)
-  |  The Directed Studies
should have a strong component matching the theme of a particular
concentration in order to fulfill the requirement of that concentration
area.
-  |  If a student has
taken SOCI4410 and 4420, and both are of the same theme that can be
counted towards the fulfillment of the requirement of one concentration
only, then only up to 3 units from either one course can be counted
towards the fulfillment of the concentration requirement, also subject to
the course supervisor’s endorsement.
-  |  If a student has
taken SOCI4410 and 4420 with different themes, subject to the course
supervisor’s endorsement, the units earned from SOCI4410 can be counted
towards the fulfillment of the requirement of one concentration and
SOCI4420 towards another.
-  |  Units earned from a
directed studies course can only be counted towards the fulfillment of
the requirement of one concentration.
[e]  |  Students
are required to take either SOCI3227 or 3236 in order to fulfill the
requirement of Required Courses. If a student has taken both SOCI3227 and
3236, the extra 3 units earned can be counted towards the fulfillment of
the requirement of Elective Courses.
[f]  |  Students
are required to take either SOCI3002 or 3241 or 4204 in order to fulfill
the requirement of Required Courses. If a student has taken more than one
of these three courses, the extra 3 units or 6 units earned can be
counted towards the fulfillment of the requirement of Elective Courses.
Requirements for admission to the 2nd degree programme:
1.  |  Students must receive first or upper second honours
in their first degree with a Major GPA of 3.3 or above; and
2.  |  Students should normally have passed all their
undergraduate courses. Students who have any failure grades on their
first degree (i.e. Bachelor of Social Science (Sociology)) transcript
will not be permitted to proceed to the second degree (i.e. Juris Doctor
(JD)) without the consent of the Programme Director of the JD Programme.
2nd Degree: Juris
Doctor ( JD )
Programme Requirement
Students are required to
complete a minimum of 72 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required Courses:  |  15 or 18
LAWS6001, 6004, 6006[a], 6007, 6901 or 6902[b]
2.  |  Elective Courses:  |  54 or 57
Students are required to take at least 54 or 57 units
of courses from the following[c]:
-  |  Elective courses which are pre-requisites for
subsequent admission to the Postgraduate Certificate in Laws (PCLL)
Programme: LAWS6011, 6013, 6015, 6017, 6018, 6019, 6020, 6021, 6022,
6023, 6024
-  |  Other elective courses offered under the Juris Doctor
Programme
-  |  Elective courses offered under the Master of Laws
Programmes
Total:  |  72
Other Requirements
1.  |  Students must also fulfill
the following requirements for graduation:
(a)  |  Students must fulfill the Term Assessment
Requirement. A student who obtains a cumulative GPA below 2.0 in the
preceding term will be put on academic probation. For details, please
refer to Clause 13.0 “Unsatisfactory Performance and Discontinuation of
Studies” of the General Regulations Governing Postgraduate Studies which
can be accessed from the Graduate School Homepage:
http://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/.
(b)  |  Students must have a cumulative GPA of at least 2.0
in order to graduate.
Explanatory Notes:
[a]  |  Students may take LAWS6089 to substitute LAWS6006.
[b]  |  Students are required to take either LAWS6901 (3-unit
course) or 6902 (6-unit course). The extra 3 units completed in LAWS6902
will count towards the total units required for graduation.
[c]  |  LAWS6012 and 6014 will no longer be pre-requisites
for admission to the PCLL Programme. Starting from the 2021-22 academic year, they will
still be elective courses offered under the JD Programme.
